topics.title: Super Cappuccino Crested Geckos: Scam or Legitimate Morph?

angleIdeas: Breeders call super cappuccino 'a scam'—but the incomplete-dominant genetics say it should be viable. Here's what the community is actually afraid of.
Two cappuccino geckos bred together don't always produce super cappuccinos. Most buyers assume they will. Here's why that matters for your breeding plan.
The cappuccino controversy isn't really about the morph. It's about whether breeders should be chasing homozygous animals at all.
Super cappuccino exists genetically, but breeding for it has become taboo. Here's the real cost—financial and ethical—of trying.

angleIdeas: Soft Scale is incomplete dominant—breed two hets and you might expect 25% double-scaled, but most breeders don't understand what a Super Soft Scale should actually look like, so they're keeping and selling normals as 'het'.
